rationale: TISAX DP policy requirement mirrors ISO 27701 clause 5.3.2 which mandates top-management-issued privacy policy addressing PII processing principles.

TISAX DP policy scope covers data-protection-specific policies, which is a subset of ISO 27701 clause 6.2 guidance that extends ISO 27002 information security policies to cover PII considerations broadly.
TISAX requirement to maintain and communicate DP policies as documented information maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 5.5.4 on privacy documented information controls.
TISAX criterion for defining data protection roles and responsibilities (DPO appointment) directly corresponds to ISO 27701 clause 5.3.3 requiring defined privacy roles and responsibilities.
TISAX data protection organizational structure requirement is a subset of ISO 27701 clause 6.3 which extends ISO 27002 organizational controls to include PII-specific governance structures.

TISAX requirement to determine whether the organization acts as controller or processor maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 5.2.2 which explicitly requires determining PII controller and/or processor roles.
TISAX processing directory (Verarbeitungsverzeichnis) as controller maps 1:1 to ISO 27701 clause 7.2.8 requiring Records Related to Processing PII for controllers (Art. 30 GDPR basis).
TISAX processing register requirements when acting as processor map directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.2.6 requiring processors to maintain records related to processing PII on behalf of controllers.
TISAX requirement to document processing purposes within the register is a sub-element of ISO 27701 clause 7.2.1 which mandates identifying and documenting processing purpose as a standalone control.
TISAX obligation to include retention periods in the processing directory implements the retention documentation aspect of ISO 27701 clause 7.4.7 on categorical retention schedules.
TISAX DPIA (Datenschutz-Folgenabschätzung) requirement maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.2.5 requiring Privacy Impact Assessment for high-risk processing activities.
TISAX criterion to assess risks within the DPIA is a practical application of ISO 27701 clause 5.4.1 which requires privacy risk assessment considering PII processing at the planning stage.
TISAX requirement to execute the DPIA prior to processing corresponds to ISO 27701 clause 5.6.2 mandating operational execution of privacy risk assessments.

TISAX DPIA necessity test (is processing limited to purpose?) partially overlaps ISO 27701 clause 7.4.2 on limiting processing, but the DPIA is a broader procedural gate whereas 7.4.2 is a design principle.
TISAX criterion for establishing a legal basis for data transfers (Art. 6 GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.2.2 on identifying the lawful basis for PII processing.
TISAX requirement to document the purpose of data transfers implements the purpose-identification sub-element of ISO 27701 clause 7.2.1.
TISAX data minimisation check at point of transfer is a specific application of ISO 27701 clause 7.4.1 which requires limiting PII collection to what is relevant and necessary.

TISAX requirement to identify and document the legal basis for third-country transfers (GDPR Chapter V) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.5.1 on identifying basis for PII transfer between jurisdictions.

TISAX obligation to maintain a list of countries and international organisations to which PII may be transferred maps 1:1 to ISO 27701 clause 7.5.2 on documented destination countries and organisations.
TISAX third-country transfer requirements when acting as processor mirror ISO 27701 clause 8.5.1 requiring processors to inform customers of PII transfers across jurisdictions.
TISAX processor-side obligation to list recipient countries maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.2 requiring processors to maintain accurate lists of destination countries for PII transfers.
TISAX requirement for AV-Vertrag (data processing agreement with processors per Art. 28 GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.2.6 on Contracts with PII Processors.
TISAX criterion for joint-controller arrangements (gemeinsame Verantwortung, Art. 26 GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.2.7 on Joint PII Controller arrangements.
TISAX requirement to maintain records of disclosures and data-sharing to third parties maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.5.3 on Records of Transfer of PII.
TISAX data-sharing log requirement is a subset of ISO 27701 clause 7.5.4 which additionally requires logging date, time and recipient identity for each PII disclosure.
TISAX requirement for sub-processor agreements (sub-AVV, Art. 28(4) GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.7 on binding agreements with subcontractors engaged to process PII.
TISAX obligation to disclose sub-processors to the controller before processing maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.6 on disclosing subcontractors used to process PII.
TISAX requirement to notify the controller in advance of any sub-processor change maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.8 on notifying customers of changes to subcontractors.
TISAX requirement to determine and document all GDPR data-subject rights obligations maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.1 on determining and fulfilling obligations to PII principals.
TISAX transparency obligation (privacy notice, Art. 13/14 GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.2 on determining the information to be provided to PII principals.
TISAX criterion for actively communicating privacy information to data subjects maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.3 on providing information to PII principals.
TISAX requirement for a consent-modification mechanism (Art. 7(3) GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.4 on providing a mechanism to modify or withdraw consent.
TISAX requirement for a documented objection mechanism (Art. 21 GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.5 on providing a mechanism to object to PII processing.
TISAX data-subject access, rectification, and erasure rights handling (Art. 15-17 GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.6 on access, correction and/or erasure mechanisms.
TISAX requirement to handle data-subject requests within legal deadlines maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.9 on handling requests from PII principals in a timely manner.

TISAX processor-side obligation to support controller with data-subject rights requests maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.3.1 requiring processors to assist the controller in fulfilling obligations to PII principals.
TISAX data-portability requirement (Art. 20 GDPR) is a specific right addressed by ISO 27701 clause 7.3.10 on data portability, but TISAX scopes it to B2B processor context narrowing applicability.

TISAX data-breach handling and notification requirement is a subset of ISO 27701 clause 6.13 which extends ISO 27002 incident management to privacy incidents, adding PII-specific responsibilities and procedures.

TISAX obligation to notify affected third parties after a breach involving shared PII maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 7.3.7 on controllers' obligations to inform third parties of rectification/erasure/restrictions.
TISAX processor requirement to document all PII disclosures including breach-related disclosures maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.3 on records of PII disclosure to third parties.
TISAX processor obligation to notify the controller of any legally-binding disclosure request maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.4 on notification of PII disclosure requests.
TISAX requirement to object to unlawful disclosure requests and consult the controller maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.5.5 on handling legally binding PII disclosures.

TISAX requirement to ensure staff handling personal data have adequate data-protection competence maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 5.5.1 on determining and maintaining privacy competence requirements.
TISAX criterion for regular data-protection awareness training maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 5.5.2 on ensuring privacy awareness for all relevant personnel.
TISAX HR data-protection onboarding and offboarding requirements are a subset of ISO 27701 clause 6.4 which extends ISO 27002 human resource security controls to PII-handling roles.

TISAX requirement for personnel to acknowledge data-protection obligations (Datenschutzverpflichtung) during employment maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 6.4.2 on privacy obligations during employment.

TISAX criterion for revoking PII access rights and enforcing confidentiality commitments at employment termination maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 6.4.3 on termination and change of employment procedures for privacy.
TISAX internal communication of data-protection obligations is a subset of ISO 27701 clause 5.5.3 which requires broader privacy communication processes covering all relevant stakeholder groups.

TISAX requirement for documented processing instructions from controller to processor (Weisungsgebundenheit, Art. 28(3)(a) GDPR) maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.2.1 on customer agreement with documented processing instructions.
TISAX requirement that processor immediately notify controller when instructions may be unlawful maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.2.4 on handling infringing instructions from the controller.

TISAX criterion restricting processor from processing PII for its own purposes beyond controller instructions maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.2.2 prohibiting the processor from determining its own processing purposes.

TISAX prohibition on processor using PII for marketing or advertising without explicit controller authorisation maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.2.3 on restrictions for marketing and advertising use of PII.

TISAX requirement for processor to contractually support controller in meeting its own GDPR obligations maps directly to ISO 27701 clause 8.2.5 on processor assisting with customer (controller) obligations.

TISAX instruction-binding requirement overlaps ISO 27701 clause 7.2.3 on when and how consent is obtained, but 9.8.1 focuses on controller-to-processor instruction flow rather than controller-to-data-subject consent mechanics.
